description: Letter from Yehuda b. Ismāʿīl al-Andalusī, Sicily, to Nissim b. ʿAyyāsh, Fustat. Dating: ca. 1060. Mentions a shipment of shelled almonds and lāsīn silk sent from Sicily in the ship of Ibn al-Baʿbāʿ and its distribution to its owners in Fustat. Also mentions selling pepper that was sent to Sicily. Ismāʿīl, Yehuda’s father, is mentioned in the blessing for the people who passed away. (Information from Gil, Kingdom, Vol. 3, #577) VMR
tags: pepper, Ibn al-Ba'ba', almonds, commodities, silk
description_he: מכתב מיהודה בן ישמעאל אל-אנדלוסי, סיציליה, לניסים בן עיאש, פוסטאט, כ-1060, על שקדים ומשי מסיציליה ועל פלפל
